personalities.description: Consistent contributor, rarely misses payments. Has stable income and strong savings discipline. Trusts the group.
First time in a savings circle. Excited but may lose motivation after a few months if they don't see their payout soon.
Wants to save but lives paycheck to paycheck. High dropout risk when unexpected expenses hit. Yield income could be the difference.
Has been burned before by circles that collapsed. Needs proof the system works. Will stay if they see others contributing consistently.
Joins hoping to get an early payout then bounce. Low commitment, high self-interest. The one who ruins circles.
The organizer who recruited everyone. Feels personally responsible if the circle fails. Highly reliable and socially invested — their reputation is on the line.
Has decent income but wants their payout NOW. Reliability is fine early on but drops sharply if they haven't received their payout by round 5. Gets restless and resentful.
Decisions are almost entirely shaped by what others do. If everyone contributes, they contribute. If people start dropping out, they panic and follow. No strong personal conviction.
Extremely disciplined with money. Low risk tolerance, very high reliability. Motivated by seeing exact numbers — yield visibility strongly influences them.
Gig worker or freelancer with unpredictable income. Some months they can pay easily, others they literally can't. Motivation is high but income stability is very low.
